title: Rank-Preserving Ensemble With Leakage-Aware CV
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, so diversified models and validation design matter more than probability calibration.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, so diversified models and validation design matter more than probability calibration. Random row splits can inflate scores because adjacent laps from the same driver-race are highly correlated.
implementation_hint: Train several models on the same engineered table: AutoGluon GBM/XGB, standalone CatBoost with categorical columns, and a regularized logistic/hazard baseline. Compare random CV to GroupKFold by Race-Year or Driver-Race, then blend predictions by OOF AUC rank or simple average; avoid isotonic unless optimizing calibration separately.
expected_effect: May improve public/private robustness by combining nonlinear tabular fit with strategy-prior and hazard-style rankings.
risk: Grouped CV may show lower local scores than random split; if public test resembles random rows, conservative validation may underselect leaderboard-tuned features.
